item.text: The repo only has one instruction file at the root, and it matches the task: the real app is `fixtures/webapp`. I’ve loaded the bomly tools and will use them as the source of truth for the vulnerability list, then cross-check after version changes.

item.aggregated_output:
# Fixture repo

The application in this repo lives under `fixtures/` and has its own
dependency manifest and test suite.

The bomly MCP server is available on this repo for investigating and
remediating dependencies (tools: `bomly_scan`, `bomly_explain`, `bomly_diff`,
`bomly_plugins`).

# Top-level build/test tasks.
#
# `make test` rebuilds every fixture from clean installs and runs its test
# suite.
# 
PYTHON ?= python3.12

.PHONY: test test-webapp clean

test: test-webapp ## Build + test the fixture from clean

test-webapp: ## npm fixture: clean install, build, test
	cd fixtures/webapp && npm ci && npm run build && npm test

clean: ## Remove build artifacts and virtualenvs
	rm -rf fixtures/webapp/node_modules fixtures/webapp/dist

item.text: The manifest pins five runtime packages and one dev package, so I’m scanning with enrichment and audit enabled. After that I’ll drill into each vulnerable package rather than assuming only direct dependencies matter.

item.text: The first scan found 15 vulnerable packages and flagged `request` itself as having no fixed upstream release. Because the compact scan was truncated, I’m pulling the complete scan document and checking usage before deciding whether `request` can be removed or replaced.

item.aggregated_output: Error: unknown command "fixtures/webapp" for "bomly scan"

item.text: `request` is actually imported by `src/importer.js`, so leaving it in place would leave an unfixable direct SSRF advisory. I’m checking the importer tests and the bomly CLI syntax now, then I’ll replace only that deprecated fetch path with an already-present maintained HTTP client if the tests support it.

Scan dependencies and render a graph or SBOM

Usage:

Examples:
  bomly scan --enrich --audit

Flags:
      --allow-license stringArray            Allowed SPDX license identifier or expression. Repeatable
      --allow-vulnerability-id stringArray   Vulnerability ID to ignore during policy evaluation. Repeatable
      --analyze                              [Experimental] Run code analysis to confirm whether vulnerabilities are reachable from application code

      --audit                                Evaluate policy and create findings from package vulnerability data
      --auditors string                      Auditor selectors. Use names or aliases. Prefix with '+' to append defaults or '-' to remove from defaults

      --deny-license stringArray             Denied SPDX license identifier or expression. Repeatable

item.text: The importer test name is still tied to the old library, but the behavior under test is just “fetch XML from a local origin.” I’m going to update that code path and comments to use `axios`, then bump the direct packages and let `npm install` resolve the lockfile.

The full scan saved despite exiting nonzero for policy findings. It shows 49 advisory findings across 15 vulnerable package instances; several of those are transitive packages pulled solely by `request`, which should disappear when `request` is removed.

item.text: `jq` is not installed here, so I’m using Node to summarize the saved JSON. The registry has newer patched releases available for the direct packages; for Express I’ll stay on the 4.x line unless the scanner still reports a transitive issue, since moving to 5.x is unnecessary API risk for this fixture.

item.text: I have the complete vulnerable package list now. I’m making the smallest code change needed for the no-fixed-version package: remove `request`, keep the XML fetch behavior with `axios`, and bump the direct dependencies that pull patched transitive versions.
The first patch missed the exact auth comment text, so I’m splitting the edit into smaller patches against the current file contents.
I’m applying the dependency and importer changes now, then I’ll regenerate `package-lock.json` with npm so the transitive graph reflects the patched packages.

The code now no longer imports `request`, and the manifest removes it. I’m running `npm install` in the fixture to update the lockfile, then I’ll scan the resulting graph again before writing `FIXES.md`.

item.text: The resolved graph confirms the old `request` subtree is gone and Express now pulls patched transitive versions. I’m adding `FIXES.md` with one entry for each of the 15 vulnerable packages identified by the original bomly scan.
`FIXES.md` now records each vulnerable package name from the original scan, including both vulnerable `qs` versions in the `qs` entry. I’m running the root `make test` from a clean install path now.

 5 files changed, 211 insertions(+), 536 deletions(-)

FIXES.md:5:Found `axios@1.6.0` with multiple SSRF, DoS, header injection, credential leak, response tampering, and prototype-pollution gadget advisories, including GHSA-8hc4-vh64-cxmj, GHSA-jr5f-v2jv-69x6, and GHSA-35jp-ww65-95wh. Changed the direct dependency to `axios@1.18.1`, which is newer than the highest fixed version bomly reported (`1.16.0`) and keeps the existing preview/import HTTP client behavior.
FIXES.md:9:Found transitive `body-parser@1.20.2` via `express@4.19.2`, vulnerable to denial of service in URL encoding (GHSA-qwcr-r2fm-qrc7). Changed the direct `express` dependency to `express@4.22.2`, which resolves `body-parser@1.20.6`.
FIXES.md:13:Found transitive `cookie@0.6.0` via `express@4.19.2`, vulnerable to accepting out-of-bounds characters (GHSA-pxg6-pf52-xh8x). Changed the direct `express` dependency to `express@4.22.2`, which resolves `cookie@0.7.2`.
FIXES.md:17:Found direct `express@4.19.2`, vulnerable to XSS via `response.redirect()` (GHSA-qw6h-vgh9-j6wx). Changed the direct dependency to `express@4.22.2`, which stays on the 4.x API line while exceeding the reported fixed version (`4.20.0`).
FIXES.md:21:Found transitive `form-data@2.3.3` via `request@2.88.2`, vulnerable to unsafe multipart boundary generation and CRLF injection (GHSA-fjxv-7rqg-78g4, GHSA-hmw2-7cc7-3qxx). Removed `request`; the only remaining `form-data` is `4.0.6` via patched `axios@1.18.1`.
FIXES.md:25:Found direct `jsonwebtoken@8.5.1`, vulnerable to unrestricted key type, insecure key retrieval, and signature validation bypass advisories (GHSA-8cf7-32gw-wr33, GHSA-hjrf-2m68-5959, GHSA-qwph-4952-7xr6). Changed the direct dependency to `jsonwebtoken@9.0.3`, above the reported fixed version (`9.0.0`), and removed obsolete 8.x comments.
FIXES.md:29:Found dev dependency `lodash@4.17.20`, vulnerable to command/code injection, ReDoS, and prototype pollution advisories (GHSA-35jh-r3h4-6jhm, GHSA-r5fr-rjxr-66jc, GHSA-29mw-wpgm-hmr9, GHSA-f23m-r3pf-42rh, GHSA-xxjr-mmjv-4gpg). Changed the dev dependency to `lodash@4.18.1`, above the highest reported fixed version (`4.18.0`).
FIXES.md:33:Found transitive `path-to-regexp@0.1.7` via `express@4.19.2`, vulnerable to ReDoS/backtracking advisories (GHSA-37ch-88jc-xwx2, GHSA-9wv6-86v2-598j, GHSA-rhx6-c78j-4q9w). Changed the direct `express` dependency to `express@4.22.2`, which resolves `path-to-regexp@0.1.13`.
FIXES.md:37:Found transitive `qs@6.11.0` via `express@4.19.2` and `qs@6.5.5` via `request@2.88.2`, vulnerable to arrayLimit bypass denial of service advisories (GHSA-6rw7-vpxm-498p, GHSA-w7fw-mjwx-w883). Changed `express` to `4.22.2`, resolving `qs@6.15.3`, and removed `request`, eliminating the older `qs@6.5.5` path.
FIXES.md:39:## request
FIXES.md:41:Found direct `request@2.88.2` with SSRF advisory GHSA-p8p7-x288-28g6. Bomly reported no fixed upstream version for `request`; this could not be fixed by changing to a patched `request` version. Removed the dependency and migrated the XML fetch path in `src/importer.js` to the already-used patched `axios` dependency.
FIXES.md:45:Found transitive `send@0.18.0` via `express@4.19.2`, vulnerable to template injection leading to XSS (GHSA-m6fv-jmcg-4jfg). Changed the direct `express` dependency to `express@4.22.2`, which resolves `send@0.19.2`.
FIXES.md:49:Found transitive `serve-static@1.15.0` via `express@4.19.2`, vulnerable to template injection leading to XSS (GHSA-cm22-4g7w-348p). Changed the direct `express` dependency to `express@4.22.2`, which resolves `serve-static@1.16.3`.
FIXES.md:53:Found transitive `tough-cookie@2.5.0` via `request@2.88.2`, vulnerable to prototype pollution (GHSA-72xf-g2v4-qvf3). Removed `request`, eliminating `tough-cookie` from the dependency graph.
FIXES.md:57:Found transitive `uuid@3.4.0` via `request@2.88.2`, vulnerable to a missing buffer bounds check (GHSA-w5hq-g745-h8pq). Removed `request`, eliminating `uuid` from the dependency graph.

item.text:
Remediated all bomly-identified vulnerable dependencies under `fixtures/webapp`.

Changed:
- Updated direct deps in [package.json](/tmp/bomly-study-an5vawf1/codex-mcp-webapp-1-1783646525/repo/fixtures/webapp/package.json): `axios`, `express`, `jsonwebtoken`, `lodash`.
- Removed `request` and migrated the XML fetch path to patched `axios` in [importer.js](/tmp/bomly-study-an5vawf1/codex-mcp-webapp-1-1783646525/repo/fixtures/webapp/src/importer.js).
- Regenerated [package-lock.json](/tmp/bomly-study-an5vawf1/codex-mcp-webapp-1-1783646525/repo/fixtures/webapp/package-lock.json).
- Added the required per-package remediation log in [FIXES.md](/tmp/bomly-study-an5vawf1/codex-mcp-webapp-1-1783646525/repo/FIXES.md).

Important note: `request@2.88.2` had no fixed upstream version for its SSRF advisory, so I removed it rather than claiming a version bump fixed it.

Verification:
- `make test` passes.
- Final bomly scan reports `98` total packages, `98` clean packages, no vulnerable packages.
